skill.name: marp-slide
skill.description: Create polished Marp presentation decks using seven embedded themes, layout guidance, image patterns, and presentation design best practices.
skill.summary: Create professional Marp slide decks with themed templates and design guidance.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection. The high static score is mostly false positives from Markdown examples, CSS theme tokens, official documentation URLs, and Marp CLI usage examples; the main real concern is minor network dependence from Google Fonts and linked documentation.

low_findings.title: External Font and Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: templates and references include hardcoded URLs, including Google Fonts imports and official Marp documentation links. This is a minor privacy and availability concern when rendered or followed, but there is no evidence of data exfiltration or malicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly present and serve fonts or official Marp resources. The destinations are expected for a presentation skill and do not receive secrets in the reviewed content.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the external command findings occur in documentation examples and fenced Markdown or shell snippets for Marp CLI usage. The skill does not include executable scripts or code that automatically runs these commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are instructional text or examples, not runnable program logic. A user or agent could choose to run Marp CLI later, but no hidden execution path was found.

low_findings.title: GitHub Token Placeholder in CI Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the env_access finding is a standard GitHub Actions placeholder inside a publishing example. It references the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN but does not read, print, transmit, or otherwise access a real secret from skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token reference appears only in a YAML example for deploying Marp output to GitHub Pages. No executable skill file or exfiltration sink uses the token.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Blocker Findings in Benign Slide Content
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: weak cryptography, C2 keyword, and reconnaissance findings match benign substrings, CSS values, Markdown syntax, or presentation wording. I found no cryptographic implementation, command-and-control behavior, host probing, or network reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are theme color variables, font declarations, and Marp directive tables. They explain slide formatting and do not perform security-sensitive operations.

content.value_statement: Presentation drafts often need clear structure and consistent visual design. This skill turns topics, notes, and rough outlines into Marp slide decks with suitable themes and layout guidance.
actual_capabilities: Selects from seven bundled Marp themes for general, minimal, colorful, dark, gradient, tech, and business decks.
Builds Markdown slide files with embedded CSS that can be used by Marp tools.
Applies slide design guidance for concise titles, bullet density, whitespace, and visual hierarchy.
Supports Marp image placement patterns, including side images, backgrounds, sizing, and filters.
Provides references for Marp syntax, custom theme CSS, official themes, and advanced features.
Saves generated decks as Markdown files in the configured outputs directory.
limitations: It creates Marp Markdown files, not finished speaker coaching or live presentation delivery.
Export to PDF, HTML, or PowerPoint depends on external Marp tooling being available.
Image quality depends on user-provided image files and valid relative paths.
Some examples reference online fonts and official documentation links that need network access when used.

use_cases.title: Prepare Training Slides
use_cases.description: Turn lesson notes into a readable Marp deck with an agenda, topic slides, and a summary slide.

use_cases.title: Create Technical Talks
use_cases.description: Build programming or architecture presentations with a tech theme and code-friendly visual style.

use_cases.title: Draft Business Presentations
use_cases.description: Create proposal, report, or results decks with a formal business theme and clear structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a topic and audience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Marp presentation about [topic] for [audience]. Use an appropriate built-in theme and save the Markdown deck.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using rough source material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these notes into a polished Marp deck. Choose a theme, split dense content into slides, and keep each slide focused: [notes].

prompt_templates.title: Design With Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Marp deck for [topic] using these images: [image paths]. Use side-image and background-image patterns where they improve clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Build Custom Presentation Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Marp presentation for [event]. Use a [tone] style, adapt the theme if needed, include speaker-ready section flow, and document any export assumptions.

output_examples.input: Create slides about cloud computing basics for new engineers.
output_examples.output: A Marp Markdown deck with a title slide, agenda, core concept slides, and a summary. The default or tech theme is selected based on tone.

output_examples.input: Make my Python tutorial look cool.
output_examples.output: A structured tutorial deck using the tech theme, concise headings, code-friendly styling, and slide breaks for each major concept.

output_examples.input: Create a Q1 results presentation for leadership.
output_examples.output: A business-themed deck with a formal title slide, metrics-focused content slides, and a closing summary for executive review.

best_practices: Provide the audience, presentation length, and desired tone before asking for slides.
Keep each slide focused on one message with three to five short supporting points.
Give local image paths and explain the purpose of each image when requesting visual layouts.
anti_patterns: Do not paste long reports and expect one slide to contain every detail.
Do not mix several unrelated visual styles in one deck without a clear reason.
Do not depend on export formats unless Marp CLI or another compatible renderer is available.

faq.question: What output does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Marp-compatible Markdown slide decks with embedded CSS themes.

faq.question: Can it export slides to PDF or PowerPoint?
faq.answer: It documents Marp export commands, but actual export requires Marp CLI or a compatible tool.

faq.question: Which themes are included?
faq.answer: It includes default, minimal, colorful, dark, gradient, tech, and business themes.

faq.question: Does it support images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Marp image patterns for backgrounds, side layouts, sizing, filters, and comparisons.

faq.question: Can it improve vague slide requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It infers a suitable theme and applies design rules for structure, spacing, and concise text.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Generated templates can reference Google Fonts, and documentation links point to official Marp resources.
